1286	[Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate]
1287	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1288	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1289	  % NOTE (iter-003): tensor-order convention. The Lean uses Mathlib's `pullbackSpecIso`
1290	  % orientation `A ⊗[R] R'` (so the codomain is `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` and the generator
1291	  % goes `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`); the prose below writes `R' ⊗_R A`. The two are the
1292	  % same module up to `TensorProduct.comm` — a faithful re-orientation, no content change.
1293	  \uses{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv, lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1294	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1295	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
1296	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1297	  % L933--938).
1298	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
1299	  % equality
1300	  % $$
1301	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
1302	  % $$
1303	  % as $R'$-modules."
1304	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
1305	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1306	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms and \(M\) an
1307	  \(A\)-module. There is a bundled \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
1308	  \[
1309	    \operatorname{regroup} :
1310	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
1311	  \]
1312	  the ``equality as \(R'\)-modules'' of the source, sending the generator
1313	  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\); equivalently its inverse sends
1314	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
1315	  hypothesis.
1316	\end{lemma}
1317	
1318	\begin{proof}
1319	  \uses{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv, lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1320	  \leanok
1321	  The underlying \(R'\)-linear equivalence is supplied, already as a bundled
1322	  \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv}\) over \(R'\), by the standalone geometry-free helper
1323	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}, whose construction is now the
1324	  natively \(R'\)-linear pushout-cancellation equivalence
1325	  \(\operatorname{Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange}\)
1326	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}). Crucially, that helper bundles
1327	  \(R'\)-linearity \emph{internally}: there is no hand-written scalar-compatibility
1328	  obligation, no induction over elementary tensors, and in particular \emph{no} residual
1329	  \(r' \cdot 0 = 0\) bookkeeping branch. The only work left to this proof is therefore to
1330	  promote that \(R'\)-linear equivalence to a morphism of \(R'\)-module \emph{objects} in
1331	  the exact \(\operatorname{extendScalars}/\operatorname{restrictScalars}\) packaging that
1332	  the codomain and domain reads produce. We work in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation
1333	  (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to the canonical commutativity isomorphism,
1334	  per the tensor-order convention note).
1335	
1336	  \emph{The lone residual obstruction is the \(A\)-action diamond, resolved at the object
1337	  level.} The pushout-cancellation helper takes its relative tensor product over the
1338	  \emph{canonical} \(A\)-algebra structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\)
1339	  (\(\operatorname{Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra}\), multiplication in the left
1340	  factor), whereas the carrier object produced by the dictionaries,
1341	  \((\operatorname{extendScalars}\iota)\,M\) with \(\iota : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) the
1342	  left inclusion, tensors over the \(\operatorname{restr}_\iota\) \(A\)-action arising
1343	  from \(\iota.\mathrm{toAlgebra}\). These two \(A\)-module structures on
1344	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) agree on elements but are distinct as data, so the two relative
1345	  tensor products are distinct types even though their underlying abelian groups and
1346	  their \(R'\)-actions coincide. This diamond is the \emph{only} thing the helper does
1347	  not already discharge, and — because the \(R'\)-linearity is now entirely internal to
1348	  the Mathlib equivalence — it is resolved purely at the carrier level by an
1349	  identity-on-elements \(R'\)-linear bridge
1350	  \[
1351	    eT : (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\operatorname{restr}} M
1352	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1353	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\mathrm{can}} M ,
1354	  \]
1355	  the identity map on generators, which is \(R'\)-linear because both sides carry the
1356	  same \(R'\)-action (restriction of scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\)).
1357	
1358	  Composing the bridge \(eT\) with the natively \(R'\)-linear helper equivalence and
1359	  promoting along \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv.toModuleIso}\) yields the desired bundled
1360	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism of \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\) objects. Being a composite
1361	  of linear equivalences it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of its constituents
1362	  carries a flatness hypothesis.
1363	\end{proof}

input.description: Read lem:base_change_map_affine_local intended signature section

content:
by the affine-open locality criterion
  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the assertion that the base-change
  map is an isomorphism over an arbitrary base-change square \((S, S', X, X')\)
  reduces to the affine--affine case \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(R)\),
  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\), \(X = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) for an \(A\)-module \(M\).

  \medskip
  \emph{Intended formalized signature.} With the ambient data
  \(f : X \to S\), \(g : S' \to S\), \(f' : X' \to S'\), \(g' : X' \to X\) and a
  proof \(h\) that the square is cartesian (\(h : \mathrm{IsPullback}\ g'\ f'\ f\ g\),
  whose commutativity \(h.w : g' \circ f = f' \circ g\) feeds the base-change map),
  under the hypotheses \([\mathrm{IsAffineHom}\ f]\) and
  \([\mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}]\), the lemma is the locality reduction
  \[
    \bigl(\forall\, U : S'.\mathrm{affineOpens},\
      \mathrm{IsIso}\ \bigl((\texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}\ f\ g\ f'\ g'\ h.w\
        \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{app}\ U\bigr)\bigr)
    \;\Longrightarrow\;
    \mathrm{IsIso}\ \bigl(\texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}\ f\ g\ f'\ g'\ h.w\
      \mathcal{F}\bigr),
  \]
  i.e.\ it discharges the affine-open locality criterion
  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} for the base-change map, with the
  per-affine-open hypothesis being exactly the affine--affine section assertion that
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange} supplies. The content
  beyond the bare locality criterion is the identification of the restriction of
  \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap} to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R')
  \subseteq S'\) with the base-change map of the affine--affine square cut out over a
  chosen affine \(\operatorname{Spec}(R) \subseteq S\) containing \(g(U)\), over which
  \([\mathrm{IsAffineHom}\ f]\) makes \(X\) restrict to \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and
  \([\mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}]\) makes \(\mathcal{F}\) restrict to
  \(\widetilde{M}\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}
[The inner comparison value \(\rho\) of Seam 2]
  \label{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_inner_value}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value, lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}
  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be homomorphisms of commutative
  rings, let \(M\) be an \(A\)-module, and write
  \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) (\(a \mapsto a \otimes 1\)) and
  \(\iota_{R'} : R' \to A \otimes_R R'\) (\(r' \mapsto 1 \otimes r'\)) for the
  canonical tensor inclusions
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}). The \emph{inner comparison
  value} is the canonical \(R\)-linear map
  \[
    \rho : M \longrightarrow (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M,
    \qquad m \longmapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m,
  \]
  realized as a morphism of \(R\)-modules from \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) to the
  threefold change-of-rings repackaging
  \(\operatorname{restr}_\psi \operatorname{restr}_{\iota_{R'}}
    \operatorname{extend}_{\iota_A} M\) of its codomain (the codomain read). Concretely it
  is the restriction of scalars \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi\) of the algebraic unit
  \(\eta_M : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of the
  extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\iota_A\) (the value identified by
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}), transported across the ring equality
  \(\iota_A \circ \varphi = \iota_{R'} \circ \psi\) — both sides being
  \(\operatorname{algMap}_R(A \otimes_R R')\) — by the change-of-rings tower
  isomorphisms. It is the value against which Seam 2
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}) identifies the
  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)-section reading of the inner leg of the base-change mate.

  Proved directly in Lean.
\end{definition}

  \(R'\)-linear, so they coincide.

  \emph{Intended formalized signature.} In the Lean affine--affine model — the square

  statement is the equality of \(R'\)-linear maps
  \[
    \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma\bigl(\texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}\
      f\ g\ f'\ g'\ h.w\ \widetilde{M}\bigr) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1}
    \;=\;
    \bigl(\texttt{TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange}\bigr)^{-1},
  \]
  where \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}} : \Gamma\bigl(g^*(f_*\widetilde{M})\bigr)
  \xrightarrow{\sim} R' \otimes_R M\) is assembled from
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso} and
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}, and
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} : \Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde{M}\bigr)
  \xrightarrow{\sim} (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) is assembled from the same two
  dictionaries; equivalently, after pre/post-composing with the dictionary isos,
  \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) \emph{is} the cancellation isomorphism. The conclusion type is a
  propositional equality of \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\)-morphisms (whence
  \(\mathrm{IsIso}\ \Gamma(\alpha)\) and \(\mathrm{IsIso}\ \alpha\) follow), not merely
  an abstract-mate identification at the level of sheaves.
\end{lemma}

content:
% for some $A$-module $M$."
  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
  change'' (proof, local-on-\(S\)-and-\(S'\) step).}
  Let \(f : X \to S\) be an affine morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, with a base-change square as above. The formation of
  the base-change map \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}
  (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}) is compatible with restriction
  to affine opens of \(S'\): for each affine open \(U \subseteq S'\), the
  restriction of the base-change map to sections over \(U\) agrees with the
  base-change map of the square obtained by restricting along \(U\). Consequently,
  by the affine-open locality criterion
  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the assertion that the base-change
  map is an isomorphism over an arbitrary base-change square \((S, S', X, X')\)
  reduces to the affine--affine case \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(R)\),
  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\), \(X = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) for an \(A\)-module \(M\).

\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
  \leanok
  By the affine-open locality criterion
  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the morphism
  \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap} is an isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules
  as soon as its restriction to the sections over each affine open \(U \subseteq S'\)
  is an isomorphism. The only content beyond the bare criterion is to identify that
  restriction with the base-change map of the affine--affine square cut out over
  \(U\). We obtain the identification by unfolding the definition of the map and
  pushing the restriction functor through each of its building blocks. The
  compatibility this needs is \emph{not} a new coherence theorem: it is the naturality
  of the adjunction transpose, together with the standard fact that pushforward
  commutes with restriction to an open of the base. We spell this out in three steps.

  \medskip
  \emph{Step 1: unfold the map.} By
  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}, \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}
  is the image, under the \((g^*, g_*)\)-adjunction transpose — the natural bijection
  \(\operatorname{Hom}(g^*A, B) \cong \operatorname{Hom}(A, g_*B)\) — of the morphism
  \[
    f_*\mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\,f_*(\eta_{\mathcal{F}})\,}
    f_*(g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F} = g_*\,f'_*(g')^*\mathcal{F},
  \]
  where \(\eta_{\mathcal{F}} : \mathcal{F} \to (g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the unit of
  the \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction and the identification
  \(f_*(g')_* = g_*\,f'_*\) is the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward applied to the
  square's commutativity \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\). Thus the map is, by
  construction, the transpose of the \(f_*\)-image of a unit — a composite of
  adjunction data and pushforward functors, with no ingredient beyond these.

  \medskip

content:
%         (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M).hom
  %         = (base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv
  In the affine--affine model (\(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\),
  \(f = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\), \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde M\) for an \(A\)-module
  \(M\)), write \(\Gamma(\theta)\) for the section-level incarnation of the base-change
  map \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}
  (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}). Conjugate it through the two reads:
  the domain read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read})
  identifies its source \(\Gamma\bigl(g^*(f_*\widetilde M)\bigr)\) with \(R' \otimes_R M\),
  and the codomain read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) identifies its target
  \(\Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\bigr)\) with \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\). Then
  the conjugated map
  \[
    \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\theta) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} :
    R' \otimes_R M \longrightarrow (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M
  \]
  is the \(R'\)-base change of the algebraic unit of extension of scalars along
  \(A \to A \otimes_R R'\), namely \(\eta_M : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\),
  \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\): explicitly it is the unique \(R'\)-linear map
  sending \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \eta_M(m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\)
  (i.e.\ \(\operatorname{LinearMap.lTensor} R'\,\eta_M\) followed by the \(R'\)-action on
  the target). Equivalently, this conjugated map is the \emph{inverse} of the regrouping
  isomorphism Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv},
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\theta) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1}
  = \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\), and in particular an isomorphism with \emph{no}
  flatness hypothesis.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv,
        lem:base_change_mate_unit_value, lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex,
        lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}
        \leanok
  The identity is now the chaining of the three seams into which the LHS unwinding of
  \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap} has been decomposed. By the \((g^* \dashv g_*)\)-transpose
  factorization of Seam~3 (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}, the counit form
  \texttt{Adjunction.homEquiv\_counit}) the section-level base-change map equals
  \(\Gamma\bigl(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\bigr) \circ \Gamma(\varepsilon_g)\), where
  \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) is the inner pushforward comparison; substituting this into the
  conjugate \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} \circ \Gamma(\theta) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
  reduces the goal to the section-level equation of Seam~3,
  \[
    \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} \circ
      \Gamma\bigl(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\bigr) \circ \Gamma(\varepsilon_g) \circ
      \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} = \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}.
  \]
  Seam~3 in turn reads the right-hand factor as the extension of scalars along
  \(\psi : R \to R'\) of the \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)-section reading \(\rho\) of
  \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) (Seam~2, Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}), and
  Seam~2 computes \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) from the affine unit value
  (Seam~1, Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}). Chaining, on the generator
  \(r' \otimes m\) the conjugated map returns \(r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) =
  (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\), using that the \(R'\)-action on \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\)
  is restriction of scalars along \(r' \mapsto 1 \otimes r'\). This is exactly the inverse of
  the regrouping isomorphism (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on generators;
  both maps are \(R'\)-linear and agree on generators \(1 \otimes m\), so they coincide.
  This is the ``boils down to the equality''
  \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M\) of the source, with no flatness hypothesis
  used anywhere.

content:
\leanok
  By the affine-open locality criterion
  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the morphism
  \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap} is an isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules
  as soon as its restriction to the sections over each affine open \(U \subseteq S'\)
  is an isomorphism. The only content beyond the bare criterion is to identify that
  restriction with the base-change map of the affine--affine square cut out over
  \(U\). We obtain the identification by unfolding the definition of the map and
  pushing the restriction functor through each of its building blocks. The
  compatibility this needs is \emph{not} a new coherence theorem: it is the naturality
  of the adjunction transpose, together with the standard fact that pushforward
  commutes with restriction to an open of the base. We spell this out in three steps.

  \medskip
  \emph{Step 1: unfold the map.} By
  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}, \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}
  is the image, under the \((g^*, g_*)\)-adjunction transpose — the natural bijection
  \(\operatorname{Hom}(g^*A, B) \cong \operatorname{Hom}(A, g_*B)\) — of the morphism
  \[
    f_*\mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\,f_*(\eta_{\mathcal{F}})\,}
    f_*(g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F} = g_*\,f'_*(g')^*\mathcal{F},
  \]
  where \(\eta_{\mathcal{F}} : \mathcal{F} \to (g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the unit of
  the \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction and the identification
  \(f_*(g')_* = g_*\,f'_*\) is the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward applied to the
  square's commutativity \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\). Thus the map is, by
  construction, the transpose of the \(f_*\)-image of a unit — a composite of
  adjunction data and pushforward functors, with no ingredient beyond these.

  \medskip
  \emph{Step 2: restriction to \(U\) commutes with each building block.} Fix an affine
  open \(U \subseteq S'\) and write \(\rho_U\) for restriction to \(U\) —
  equivalently, the section functor \(\Gamma(U, -)\) on \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules,
  or pullback along the open immersion \(U \hookrightarrow S'\). Restriction along
  \(U\) is a functor, and every ingredient of Step 1 is a natural transformation or a
  natural bijection; so applying \(\rho_U\) to the transpose-of-a-unit of Step 1
  returns the transpose-of-the-unit built from the restricted data. Concretely:
  \begin{itemize}
    \item[(a)] the unit \(\eta_{\mathcal{F}}\) of the \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction
      restricts to the unit of the corresponding adjunction on the restricted square,
      because units are natural in their object and pulling the cartesian square back
      along \(U \hookrightarrow S'\) yields again a cartesian square (with total space
      the open \((f')^{-1}(U)\) of \(X'\));
    \item[(b)] \(f_*\) of a restricted morphism is the restriction of \(f_*\) of the
      morphism, because pushforward commutes with restriction to an open of the base:
      for an open \(U\) of the base one has
      \((f_*\mathcal{G})|_U = (f|_{f^{-1}U})_*\bigl(\mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}U}\bigr)\),
      naturally in \(\mathcal{G}\) — and likewise for \((g')_*\) and \(f'_*\);
    \item[(c)] the \((g^*, g_*)\)-adjunction transpose is a bijection natural in both
      variables, so it commutes with restricting its source and target along \(U\).
  \end{itemize}
  Chaining (a), (b), (c) — i.e.\ transporting the transpose-of-unit composite of
  Step 1 through the restriction functor \(\rho_U\) and replacing each block by its
  restricted counterpart — shows that
  \((\texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}\dots).\mathrm{app}\,U\), the restriction of the
  base-change map to sections over \(U\), is itself \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap},
  now formed from the cartesian square restricted over \(U\). Choosing an affine open
  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R) \subseteq S\) containing the image \(g(U)\), affineness of
  \(f\) (local on the base and stable under base change) makes the total space
  restrict to an affine \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\), and quasi-coherence of
  \(\mathcal{F}\) makes it restrict to a tilde-module \(\widetilde{M}\) there; so this
  restricted map is exactly the base-change map of the affine--affine square cut out
  over \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\) and \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\). The whole
  identification is thus definitional plus the naturalities (a)--(c); it introduces no
  obligation beyond them.

  \medskip
  \emph{Step 3: conclude the reduction.} By Step 2 the per-affine-open hypothesis
  required by the locality criterion Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} —
  that \((\texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}\dots).\mathrm{app}\,U\) is an isomorphism
  for every affine open \(U \subseteq S'\) — is, under the identification of Step 2,
  precisely the affine--affine section assertion. That assertion is supplied, with
  \emph{no} flatness hypothesis, by the section-level computation
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}, which identifies the
  affine--affine base-change map on global sections with the cancellation isomorphism
  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\). Applying the locality criterion
  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} to this family of per-open
  isomorphisms gives \(\mathrm{IsIso}(\texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}\dots)\), which
  is the asserted locality reduction.
\end{proof}

\[
    \operatorname{cancelBaseChange} :
    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M,
    \qquad (r' \otimes a) \otimes m \;\mapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m)
  \]
  (Mathlib's \texttt{TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange}; here
  \(a \cdot m\) is the \(A\)-action on \(M\)). Equivalently, in the direction in which
  \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is presented — from the source \(R' \otimes_R M\) to the target
  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) — it is \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}^{-1}\),
  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\); the two readings agree because
  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) is an isomorphism. Since
  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) is an isomorphism with \emph{no} flatness
  hypothesis, this identification closes the affine case: \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is an
  isomorphism, hence (full faithfulness / counit isomorphism of \(\widetilde{(-)}\) on
  quasi-coherent modules) so is \(\alpha\) on the affine chart.

\end{lemma}

  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\alpha) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1}\) sends

\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}
  \uses{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}
  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be homomorphisms of commutative
  rings, and regard \(R'\) and \(A\) as \(R\)-algebras via \(\psi\) and \(\varphi\).
  Over the generic pullback square
  \(\operatorname{Limits.pullback}(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi, \operatorname{Spec}\psi)\)
  the canonical isomorphism \texttt{pullbackSpecIso}
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}) identifies the total space with
  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\), and under it the two cone legs are the
  \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps of the canonical tensor inclusions:
  \[
    \operatorname{pullback.fst} \;=\; (\texttt{pullbackSpecIso}).\mathrm{hom}^{-1}
      \circ \operatorname{Spec}\bigl(A \to R' \otimes_R A\bigr),
    \qquad
    \operatorname{pullback.snd} \;=\; (\texttt{pullbackSpecIso}).\mathrm{hom}^{-1}
      \circ \operatorname{Spec}\bigl(R' \to R' \otimes_R A\bigr),
  \]
  the inclusions being \(a \mapsto 1 \otimes a\) and \(r' \mapsto r' \otimes 1\)
  (equivalently, the \texttt{\_hom\_fst} / \texttt{\_hom\_snd} / \texttt{\_inv\_fst}
  forms of those Mathlib identities).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}
  \leanok
  This is Mathlib's \texttt{pullbackSpecIso\_hom\_fst} and
  \texttt{pullbackSpecIso\_hom\_snd} (and their \texttt{\_inv} companions) transported
  to the present square. The only content beyond citing those identities is the
  structure bridging: the given morphisms \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) and
  \(\operatorname{Spec}\psi\) must be matched with the \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps of
  the \texttt{algebraMap}s for the \(R\)-algebra structures that \(\varphi\) and
  \(\psi\) define on \(A\) and \(R'\) — a definitional identification of
  \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) with
  \(\operatorname{Spec}(\texttt{CommRingCat.ofHom}\,(\texttt{algebraMap}\,R\,A))\) (and
  likewise for \(\psi\)), after which the Mathlib companion lemmas apply verbatim. This
  identification of the cone legs is the one Mathlib-absent step of the section-level
  computation; isolating it here exposes it as a small, self-contained obligation.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
